Motion For Approval Of Paga Settlement
Off Calendar Aug-11-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Atty. PATRICE C. NAGLE, Esq. of Fisher & Phillips LLP (Tel: 858-597-9600) for Defendant
American Technologies, Inc.;
No other appearances made in this matter.

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFFS JUAN FLORES and JOSE SOLIS LUGO's Motion For Approval Of PAGA Settlement.

Matter not contested/argued. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. Plaintiffs' motion for approval of PAGA settlement is granted. The Court finds that the overall amount of the proposed settlement is fair and adequate in view of the purposes and policies of PAGA, taking into account such factors as the adequacy of the civil penalties in light of the potential maximum if the matter were to go to verdict and the risks of litigation. (Lab. Code Sec 2699(l)(2); see Haralson v. U.S. Aviation Services Corp. (N.D. Cal. 2019) 383 F.Supp.3d 959, 972; Flores v. Starwood Hotels & Resorts Worldwide, Inc. (C.D. Cal. 2017) 253 F.Supp.3d 1074, 1077; O'Connor v. Uber Technologies, Inc. (N.D. Cal. 2016) 201 F.Supp.3d 1110, 1132-35.) The Court also finds that the proposed award of $74,250 in attorneys' fees, which represents 33% of the gross settlement amount of $225,000, and actual costs and expenses incurred not to exceed $15,000, is a reasonable amount in the circumstances of the case, including the result achieved, contingent risk, and counsel's lodestar. (See Laffitte v. Robert Half Int'l Inc. (2016) 1 Cal.5th 480.)

Counsel for the plaintiffs is required to prepare and submit proposed order.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Rose Miller, CSR#5041, email: RMillerCSR@gmail.com (302/EPS)
